trends.name: AI-CapEx / data center infrastructure
trends.rationale: DC buildout is explicitly named as a driver of demand for AI memory; electronics/machinery trade flows are increasing.

trends.name: AI memory / HBM as a strategic bottleneck
trends.rationale: Hynix publicly promises to ramp up production amid explosive demand; this signals the consolidation of memory as a key constraint.

trends.name: Shift of production role to SEA (electronics for AI)
trends.rationale: Thailand records a 4-year high in AI-led electronics exports/imports and strengthens the trade-political axis (agreement with the USA).

trends.name: Visual AI / multimodality in consumer devices (wearables)
trends.rationale: Apple identifies Visual Intelligence as a defining feature of wearable AI devices — a sign of a product bet.

trends.name: AI in cybercrime / AI-backed attacks
trends.rationale: The UAE speaks of a “qualitative shift” in methods (terrorist/hostile actors), and the public agenda on AI-enabled online fraud simplification is growing.

trends.name: Provenance / “what is real, what is AI” (trust infrastructure)
trends.rationale: Microsoft is advancing an online reality/AI verification plan — a signal of practice consolidation.

trends.name: Agent platforms (enterprise agents)
trends.rationale: The research loop strengthens the standardization/indexing of agent systems, but mass transparency/documentation of safety remains low (hindering adoption).

trends.name: “AI hype in public markets” (multiples without proven returns)
trends.rationale: “AI scare trade” and skepticism towards the infrastructure leader (Nvidia) are noticeable, indicating a cooling of expectations.

trends.name: [Fading] “model benchmarks” to “agent system control” as new
trends.rationale: Transition from "'model benchmarks' to 'agent control'" to ""

trends.name: AI-CapEx / data center infrastructure (debt + suppliers)
trends.rationale: Wesco bond re-subscription and institutionalization at Citi.

trends.name: “Builders > Coders” (rent shift to AI physical economy)
trends.rationale: UBS thesis becomes the mainstream investment framework, supported by profits of power/cooling suppliers.

trends.name: AI-disruption-discount in private/secondary
trends.rationale: “AI fear” is shifting to private markets, impacting liquidity and share pricing.

trends.name: Upside skepticism on the “core” (Nvidia) while maintaining infra boom
trends.rationale: public thesis of “little scope for meaningful upside” — a sign of expectation saturation, although the infrastructure chain continues to grow.

trends.name: Safety pledges / voluntary guardrails
trends.rationale: Anthropic weakens its pledge, confirming that the pledge mode cannot withstand the race.

trends.name: Infrastructure vulnerabilities of multi-tenant LLM serving (KV cache leakage)
trends.rationale: OptiLeak lowers the cost of attacks, increasing the likelihood of regulatory/corporate response (isolation/certification).

trends.name: Market pays for the “core” (Nvidia) as main source of upside.
trends.rationale: Fading: the transition from "the market pays for the 'core' (Nvidia) as the main source of upside" to "the market begins to doubt the core's upside and expands beneficiaries to the entire infrastructure belt (electricity/cooling/suppliers/debt)." Mentions in the context of the transition: 1

trends.name: “AI will eat software” — mainly a public market narrative.
trends.rationale: Fading: the shift from “AI will eat software” — primarily a public market narrative — to “risk recalibration occurs in secondary/private markets,” i.e., within the liquidity machine of venture/PE. Mentions in the context of this transition: 0.

trends.name: AI-CapEx / compute infrastructure (GPU + DC)
trends.rationale: Nvidia states that customers are “racing to invest in AI compute,” but the market reacts cautiously—growth continues but without euphoria.

trends.name: Energy constraint of AI (power-as-bottleneck)
trends.rationale: The White House is reframing the data center energy topic into a public commitment format for Big Tech — a strong institutional accelerator.

trends.name: Export control of chips / licensing of supplies to China
trends.rationale: The license for a small volume of H200 confirms that the "managed crane" mode is being established.

trends.name: Agent AI platforms (workflow agents)
trends.rationale: A surge in research on agentic RL, routing, memory, orchestration, etc., indicates a transition from “demo” to “engineering norm.”

trends.name: Security of AI agents / stateful defense
trends.rationale: A real incident of Claude abuse for hacking government agencies sharply increases demand for defense-in-depth.

trends.name: Closed API models as “single standard”
trends.rationale: On one hand, demand from government/defense is strengthening; on the other, pressure is growing due to misuse and requirements for “circuits” (civil/military), which fragments the market.

trends.name: Classic enterprise SaaS (without radical AI reassembly)
trends.rationale: Weak forecasts from Salesforce/Snowflake/Zoom and investor nervousness reflect expectations of value migration from licenses/seat-based models to agent functions/automation.

trends.name: “AI hype without results” in Chinese public markets
trends.rationale: The Baidu sell-off shows that capital in China is beginning to demand stricter proof of AI monetization.

trends.name: Global optimization of GPU/cloud procurement by price and availability.
trends.rationale: Fading: the shift from "global optimization of GPU/cloud procurement by price and availability" to a "mode of 'measured admission' through licenses, where volume and SKU are policy tools." Mentions in the context of this shift: 0

trends.name: Bottleneck = GPU/capital.
trends.rationale: Fading: the shift from "bottleneck = GPU/capital" to "bottleneck = GPU + energy capacity + politico-infrastructural agreements." Mentions in the context of the shift: 0

trends.name: Quality assessment by chat benchmarks/single answers.
trends.rationale: Fading: the shift from "quality assessment based on chat benchmarks/single responses" to "evaluation based on workflow robustness, episode cost, reliability certification, stateful protection." Mentions in the context of this shift: 0

trends.name: AI-CapEx (energy, cooling, network, DC infrastructure)
trends.rationale: Record profits in power/liquid cooling for AI data centers and expansion of fiber for Anthropic confirm acceleration of physical infrastructure.

trends.name: Energy-efficient and high-bandwidth AI chips (competition with Nvidia monoculture)
trends.rationale: Broadcom ships a new AI chip from Fujitsu with a focus on power/data transfer; simultaneously, Nvidia faces market nervousness – space for diversification is growing.

trends.name: Agentic coding / SWE agents (and “performance race”)
trends.rationale: Media "productivity panic" and scientific improvements (memory by subtasks, SLM+expert) indicate rapid adoption and competition of methodologies.

trends.name: Budget-aware orchestration / routing (economics of “cheap+expensive” models)
trends.rationale: Clear research frameworks for sequential routing under budget constraints and equilibrium economic orchestration models are shaping the future operational standard.

trends.name: Certification of reliability and “assurance layers” for agents (self-consistency + conformal etc.)
trends.rationale: A “deployment gate” language emerges with finite selective guarantees – this is a direct bridge to compliance and procurement.

trends.name: Classic SaaS rents/multiples (without radical product restructuring)
trends.rationale: Even with strong forecasts from Snowflake and support from Salesforce buybacks, the market discusses structural compression of multiples and the threat of AI disruption to software.

trends.name: AI euphoria in public markets (without disclaimers about bubble/rotation)
trends.rationale: The "AI scare" and volatility around Nvidia demonstrate a transition from euphoria to a mode of selectivity and rotation.

trends.name: “AI commerce in social networks without trust conflict”
trends.rationale: Tests like Instagram Shop the Look face resistance from creators – monetization through AI substitution may break reputational boundaries.

trends.name: China open-source / open-weight AI as a global competitive line
trends.rationale: The discourse on the trajectory of Chinese open-source AI continues to consolidate (as an alternative path to Western API models), although in the current package it is more of a background signal than “breaking.”

trends.name: Dominance of narrative about software margin and valuation growth.
trends.rationale: Fading: the transition from "dominance of the narrative about software margin and valuation growth" to "highlighting the infrastructure layer as the main beneficiary and 'safe haven'; growth in the role of power/cooling/fiber and energy-efficient chips." Mentions in the context of the transition: 0

trends.name: Large SaaS packages as coordination centers.
trends.rationale: Fading: the transition from "large SaaS suites as coordination centers" to "thin orchestrators + market of specialized agents/models, where the key is protocol/traceability/verification." Mentions in the context of the transition: 0

trends.name: Optimism “agents will do everything.”
trends.rationale: Fading: the transition from "optimism that 'agents will do everything'" to "agency as a controlled mode: cost caps, reliability gates, stateful defense." Mentions in the context of the transition: 0

trends.name: Configurations “models as software product, VC as fuel”
trends.rationale: Fading: the transition from the “model as a software product, VC as fuel” configuration to the “model as infrastructure, megafinance + chip/server + energy chains” configuration. Mentions in the context of this transition: 0

trends.name: Chatbots/assistants with heuristic guardrails
trends.rationale: Fading: the transition from "chatbots/assistants with heuristic guardrails" to "agent systems with formal contracts, traceability, evaluation protocols, memory management, and stochasticity." Mentions in the context of the transition: 0

trends.name: Tacit agreement “Big Tech serves government orders”
trends.rationale: Fading: the transition from "tacit agreement that 'Big Tech serves government orders'" to "open struggle over the boundaries of permissible AI applications (especially lethal autonomy and domestic surveillance)." Mentions in the context of the transition: 1

trends.name: AI-CapEx / data center infrastructure
trends.rationale: Blackstone is developing a public tool for data center acquisitions, while Dell forecasts a boom in AI servers.

trends.name: State defense LLM loop (classified deployments)
trends.rationale: OpenAI gains access to model deployment within the Pentagon’s classified network amid the Anthropic crisis.

trends.name: Financialization of AI through mega-rounds “for compute and talent”
trends.rationale: $110B OpenAI round at a $730B valuation – extreme concentration of capital among leaders.

trends.name: Agent “behavior contracts” and runtime enforcement of constraints
trends.rationale: ABC formalizes specification/invariants/recovery as a standard component of agent systems.

trends.name: Audit/reproducibility of agent engineering (event sourcing, replay, traceability)
trends.rationale: ESAA offers an engineering pattern compatible with forensic and compliance requirements.

trends.name: Open-weight / open-source agent frameworks for deep research
trends.rationale: MiroFlow claims SOTA on a number of agent benchmarks as an OSS alternative to expensive APIs.

trends.name: “Classic financial intermediaries under AI pressure” (banks/financial sector)
trends.rationale: Shares of financial companies are falling due to a combination of "AI threat" and credit risks – expectations of worsening rent economy.

trends.name: Crypto as 24/7 market for traditional assets (commodities-on-crypto venues)
trends.rationale: At the moment of geopolitical stress, liquidity and hedging move into the 24/7 crypto circuit.

trends.name: Infrastructure overheating/fear of overspending (compute overbuild risk)
trends.rationale: CoreWeave declines on concerns over excessive costs and expanding capex — the market demands proof of ROI.

trends.name: Models as general-purpose cloud services.
trends.rationale: Fading: the transition from "model as a general-purpose cloud service" to "model as an element of critical infrastructure in classified networks + politically managed supply chain." Mentions in the context of the transition: 2

trends.name: Compute financing through private rounds and infrastructure company debt.
trends.rationale: Fading: the shift from "compute financing through private rounds and infrastructure company debts" to "mass financialization of data centers (public structures/tools), growth of the role of 'infrastructure' investors." Mentions in the context of the shift: 4

trends.name: “Smart text response” without reproducibility and formal guarantees.
trends.rationale: Fading: the transition from “smart text response” without reproducibility and formal guarantees to “agent as an executable system with specification (contracts), event log (event sourcing), metrics/benchmarks.” Mentions in transition context: 1

trends.name: AI-CapEx / data centers as an investment class
trends.rationale: Blackstone packages a data center acquisition strategy into a public instrument; demand for AI servers confirmed by Dell.

trends.name: Market discipline towards “compute race” (fear of CapEx overheating)
trends.rationale: Market reaction to CoreWeave losses/CapEx – capital intensity begins to be penalized rather than automatically rewarded.

trends.name: Defense-grade AI and “closed loops” as a market
trends.rationale: OpenAI gains placement in a classified network; Anthropic's problems show that guardrails are a competitive factor, not just PR.

trends.name: Agent platforms (tool-using agents) in everyday life/business
trends.rationale: Alongside the growing interest, the attack surface is expanding (ClawJacked), but this usually does not stop adoption; rather, it changes architectures.

trends.name: Security of AI agents (agent security) as a mandatory layer
trends.rationale: The emergence of specific exploits makes agent tool-use security an inevitable discipline and market.

trends.name: AI-RAN / 6G as “AI-native network”
trends.rationale: Nvidia and Nokia are institutionalizing the agenda; still at an early stage, but this is a typical "slow but irreversible" standard cycle.

trends.name: “AI as a justification for layoffs” (AI-washing) and public skepticism
trends.rationale: The Block case brings the practice into public discourse; a wave of similar statements and responsive reactions (regulatory/union/reputational) is likely.

trends.name: “Unconditional bullish on Big Tech” (Big Tech as market locomotive)
trends.rationale: Bloomberg notes that Big Tech is “no longer leading stocks higher,” with growing demand for hedging via 0DTE.

trends.name: “Moral/behavioral benchmarks” for LLM (beyond math & code)
trends.rationale: this is still a research agenda, but it lays the groundwork for future compliance standards and risk assessment.

trends.name: AI-CapEx / infrastructure race (including optics/interconnect)
trends.rationale: Nvidia is investing $4 billion in data center optics; Amazon continues extreme spending on AI, even at the cost of market reaction.

trends.name: LLM reliability as a product (SLA/uptime/resilience)
trends.rationale: major Claude outage amid demand; real demand begins to “break” frontier services.

trends.name: Agentic AI in enterprise (tool-use, MCP, standardization)
trends.rationale: Datasets and metrics for MCP tool retrieval (HumanMCP) are emerging, indicating a shift towards industrialization of agent stacks.

trends.name: AI in finance (credit markets, microstructure, compliance agents)
trends.rationale: JPMorgan publicly articulates the transformation of credit traders’ labor; concurrently – TradeFM as a foundation model for microstructure.

trends.name: Sovereign/regional AI and polycentricity (China etc.)
trends.rationale: MiniMax shows 159% revenue growth; increased attention to Chinese open-source AI.

trends.name: “Vector DB as mandatory RAG layer”
trends.rationale: The work shows that agentic keyword search can deliver >90% RAG quality without a persistent vector DB, putting pressure on the market for "mandatory" vector stores in certain use cases.

trends.name: Social legitimacy of AI (growth of protests/conflicts)
trends.rationale: The largest anti-AI protest in London at a key AI hub signals an acceleration of public resistance.

trends.name: Geographical vulnerability of clouds (physical incidents/geopolitics)
trends.rationale: AWS outage after a strike on a data center in the UAE; war/logistics impact supply and operational regions.

trends.name: Dominance of narrow shortage “GPU as main limit.”
trends.rationale: Fading: the shift from "dominance of the narrow GPU shortage as the main bottleneck" to "a configuration where the bottleneck is *cluster connectivity, memory/cache, latency, fault tolerance*, making optics/interconnect and operations critical." Mentions in the context of this transition: 0

trends.name: Assessments by static benchmarks and API sales as “black box.”
trends.rationale: Fading: the transition from “evaluation by static benchmarks and selling API as a ‘black box’” to “agent stacks with standardized tools/protocols and lifecycle validation ‘in the field’.” Mentions in transition context: 1

trends.name: Local pilots and chat assistants.
trends.rationale: Fading: the shift from "local pilots and chat assistants" to "AI deployment as a process operator with access to unstructured data, simulators, and risk metrics." Mentions in the context of this transition: 1

trends.name: AI-CapEx / data centers as debt infrastructure
trends.rationale: Major data center projects are financed through high-yield debt ($2.15B) and large power contracts (500 MW) — scaling is anchored by financing and demand.

trends.name: Sovereign AI / model control (on-prem, private deployments)
trends.rationale: Mistral's public thesis about companies' desire to maintain control + embedded engineer practices under large clients.

trends.name: Resilience architectures of cloud (geo-risk aware cloud / multi-region by design)
trends.rationale: Real attacks on AWS data centers shift reliability from an SRE topic to a geopolitical risk.

trends.name: Agent Runtime Infrastructure / observability, traces, self-healing
trends.rationale: The emergence of the runtime layer as a distinct research and tooling object; growth in frameworks for execution trace analysis.

trends.name: “Verifiable agents” (constraint/tool verification, deterministic evaluation)
trends.rationale: CoVe, LOGIGEN, T^3RL represent a clear industry tilt towards verifiable trajectories and verifiable rewards to bring agents into production.

trends.name: LLM-as-a-Judge as a universal evaluation standard
trends.rationale: There is a rollback to benchmarks that exclude judge variability and measure performance directly (especially in finance).

trends.name: AI qualification of “all professions” (massive government upskilling programs)
trends.rationale: Singapore is expanding AI training to lawyers/accountants/prisoners – an indicator of the institutionalization of AI literacy as a civic competence.

trends.name: Public legitimacy of AI (social acceptance) in tech hubs
trends.rationale: largest protests - an indicator that the social environment is becoming a constraint on deployments and the labor market.

trends.name: Configurations: AI as venture + cloud as “pure service” with abstract reliability.
trends.rationale: Fading: the transition from the “configuration: AI as venture + cloud as a ‘pure service’ with abstract reliability” to the “new configuration: compute/DC as an infrastructure asset financed by debt, with geopolitical risks as a credit quality parameter.” Mentions in the context of this transition: 4

trends.name: Improvement of “answer quality” benchmarks and model scaling.
trends.rationale: Fading: the transition from "improving benchmarks of 'response quality' and scaling models" to "standardization of execution (runtime infrastructure), diagnostics of agent traces, and provable/verifiable training and evaluation loops." Mentions in the context of the transition: 0

trends.name: Model provider as platform selling API/weights.
trends.rationale: Fading: the transition from "model-provider as a platform selling API/weights" to "provider + 'field teams' implementing at the client (consulting/engineering) to retain market share under demands for model control." Mentions in the context of the transition: 0

trends.name: AI-CapEx / infrastructure (compute buildout)
trends.rationale: Large debt placements for compute expansion indicate sustained demand for capacity even amid rising uncertainty.

trends.name: Financialization of AI through high-yield / junk bonds
trends.rationale: “AI-related debt remains firm” — the establishment of the debt channel as a systemic mechanism.

trends.name: Defense-tech + AI (autonomous systems, contracts, capital)
trends.rationale: $4B round for Anduril at approximately $60B valuation amid discourse on “reshaping warfare.”

trends.name: Optical interconnect / photonics for data centers
trends.rationale: $2B Nvidia investment in Coherent - a clear bet on the next bottleneck in data centers.

trends.name: Agent observability & runtime governance (runtime infrastructure, tracing, self-healing)
trends.rationale: A distinct execution layer and diagnostic/analysis tools for agent traces are emerging as the new engineering norm.

trends.name: Verifiable/checked agent training (constraints/tools as verifier)
trends.rationale: LOGIGEN / CoVe / T^3RL – common vector: data and RL through deterministic trajectory verification.

trends.name: “Hottest AI trades” as a market narrative (momentum equity)
trends.rationale: Risk-off amid oil/inflation leads to unwind and capital outflows from the most overheated AI bets (especially in Asia).

trends.name: Prediction markets (crypto roots) as mass expectation coordination tool
trends.rationale: “Fast start running out of steam” combined with intensifying regulatory discussions/guardrails — cooling down.

trends.name: Social legitimacy of AI (protests/public conflict)
trends.rationale: The largest anti-AI protest in London indicates growing visible politicization and public mobilization.

trends.name: LLM as product/API; quality control through post-factum logs and offline evaluations.
trends.rationale: Fading: the transition from "LLM as product/API; quality control through post-factum logs and offline evaluations" to "agent as process/execution, where *runtime* (cost, reliability, security) is optimized, not just weights." Mentions in the context of the transition: 7

trends.name: AI growth mainly through equity/venture and “story stocks.”
trends.rationale: Fading: the transition from "AI growth mainly through equity/venture and 'story stocks'" to "compute-building as project financing under anchor contracts; junk bonds as a standard instrument." Mentions in the context of the transition: 3

trends.name: “AI company can control who and how uses it.”
trends.rationale: Fading: the transition from "'AI company can control who and how uses it'" to "government customer/Pentagon determines application; supplier sets contractual boundaries but does not own the solution." Mentions in the context of the transition: 0
